flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of the enqueue operation in an array-based queue implementation?
flashcards.back: O(1) - Constant time. We simply add an element at the rear index and increment the rear pointer.

flashcards.front: In array-based queue implementation, what do 'front' and 'rear' represent?
flashcards.back: front points to the index of the first (oldest) element in the queue; rear points to the index just after the last (newest) element in the queue.

flashcards.front: What is queue overflow condition in array implementation?
flashcards.back: Overflow occurs when rear equals MAXSIZE - 1 (or the last index), indicating the array is full and no more elements can be enqueued.

flashcards.front: What is queue underflow condition in array implementation?
flashcards.back: Underflow occurs when front exceeds rear (or front == -1 initially), indicating the queue is empty and no element can be dequeued.

flashcards.front: Why do linear queues implemented with arrays face wastage problem even when queue is not full?
flashcards.back: As elements are dequeued, the front index keeps increasing, but the array positions before front become unused and cannot be reused, causing memory wastage.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a linear queue and circular queue in array implementation?
flashcards.back: In linear queue, rear keeps moving forward; in circular queue, the last position connects back to the first, allowing reuse of freed positions at the front.

flashcards.front: What is the space complexity of array-based queue implementation?
flashcards.back: O(n) where n is the maximum capacity of the queue. The array of size MAXSIZE is allocated regardless of actual number of elements.

flashcards.front: What condition determines if an array-based queue is empty?
flashcards.back: Queue is empty when front == -1 (initial state) or when front > rear after multiple dequeues.

flashcards.front: What condition determines if an array-based queue is full?
flashcards.back: Queue is full when rear == MAXSIZE - 1, meaning the rear pointer has reached the last array index.

flashcards.front: After a dequeue operation in array implementation, when should we reset both front and rear?
flashcards.back: When the last element is dequeued (front == rear == MAXSIZE - 1 or after dequeue, front > rear), we should reset front = rear = -1 to indicate empty queue.

flashcards.front: In array-based queue, what happens to the front index during dequeue operation?
flashcards.back: The front index is incremented (front++) to move past the removed element, effectively discarding it from logical consideration.

flashcards.front: What are the four fundamental queue operations in array implementation?
flashcards.back: enqueue (insert at rear), dequeue (remove from front), peek/front (view front element), and isEmpty/isFull (check status).
